label: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 5

extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won the pan-flat sprint to Albacete after a major late crash decimated leadout trains and eliminated BARDET from GC contention.
tactical_patterns: PHILIPSEN won despite DÉMARE's FDJ train being fragmented by late crash, revealing vulnerability of pre-positioned leadouts to late chaos.
JAKOBSEN faded in finale after strong intermediate sprint effort, suggesting inability to double-up on long flat stages.
BikeExchange hit in crashes two consecutive days, indicating poor positioning protocol or rider fatigue affecting spatial awareness.
Breakaway containing only LAZCANO as sprinter shows teams now calculate intermediate sprint points when selecting escapees for flat stages.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: recovered from previous day's self-described mess-up to win stage and take green, showing strong mental reset ability.
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: neutral: finished second but clearly spent energy earlier, suggesting stamina limitations on 184km flat stages with double sprint efforts.
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: lost 12 minutes after crash with 22km remaining, catastrophic GC damage reveals poor positioning on supposedly routine flat stage.
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: negative: hot favorite undone when SÉNÉCHAL leadout compromised by crash recovery fatigue, questioning FDJ's Plan B sprint protocol.
rider_signals.TAARAMÄE Rein: negative: crashed second consecutive day while in leader's jersey, suggesting over-aggressive positioning or declining bike handling under pressure.
rider_signals.ELISSONDE Kenny: neutral: inherits red jersey by 5 seconds purely through Taaramäe's crash losses, not active racing strength.
